standard_variable.semantic_grain: one row per microbiology specimen-test event

database_mapping.source_grain: one opening-scope source microbiology specimen-test branch before parent-event aggregation
database_mapping.target_grain: one retained parent microbiology specimen-test event row

microbiology_family_build_translation.entity_grain_translation: aggregate source rows to one microbiology_test_event_id per source micro_specimen_id and test_seq
microbiology_family_build_translation.source_hierarchy_translation: retain source specimen-test rows as parent events and expose organism/susceptibility child counts without merging child rows into parent event rows
microbiology_family_build_translation.parent_child_translation: child organism rows link back to this parent through microbiology_test_event_id
microbiology_family_build_translation.raw_text_retention_translation: retain raw specimen description, raw test name, event comment text, and Gram-stain helper text/classes
microbiology_family_build_translation.no_row_translation: absence of a retained parent event means no approved opening-scope performed microbiology result event, not absence of infection or culture order

validation_contract.no_row_policy_check: absence of row is not proof of negative culture, no organism, no infection, no order, or no antibiotic exposure
notes: has_organism_child alone is not definitive positivity; use event_positivity_analysis_class and raw comments.
Orders-side microbiology is excluded until a separate order-result linkage rule is approved.

execution_contract.formal_output_rule: The governed execution.py entrypoint should read this mapping spec and delegate to the current local reviewed implementation.
execution_contract.non_bypassable_target_rule: Formal std_microbiology_test_event outputs for MIMIC-IV-3.1 should be produced only through the governed execution.py path.
